title: श्री राम चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री राम चालीसा ॥

॥ दोहा ॥
श्री रामचन्द्र कृपालु कर, मन निवास हमार।
आतुर आरत जन भये, प्रभु तजो मतिभ्रम भार॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय राम रूप अतुलित बानी।
जय सीतापति जग हितकारी॥
जय सुर मुनि जन सुखदायक।
जय दशरथ पुत्र प्रभु नायक॥१॥

जय कौशल्या माता के लाला।
जय दशरथ प्रिय प्रतिपाला॥
जय सुख धाम राम रघुराई।
जय लक्ष्मण प्रिय सकल सुखदाई॥२॥

नाम राम रघुनाथ कहावौ।
त्रिभुवन में प्रभुताई गावौ॥
कौशल देश मथुरा निवासी।
रघुकुल तिलक दशरथ सुत रासी॥३॥

विश्वामित्र मख रक्षा कीन्ही।
ताड़का मारि सुकीर्ति नवीन्ही॥
गौतम नारि श्राप जब पायो।
पादरज धरि उद्धार करायो॥४॥

जनकपुर में धनुष चढ़ायो।
सीता जी का पाणि गृहायो॥
भार्गव के सब गर्व उतारे।
तात वचन हित बन पगु धारे॥५॥

पंचवटी में डेरा कीन्हा।
शूर्पणखा नासिका बिहीना॥
खर दूषण त्रिशिरा सँहारे।
सब खल दल प्रभु आपु निवारे॥६॥

मारीच मृग प्रपंच रचायो।
सीता हरि दसकंध लै जायो॥
जटायू की गति प्रभु जब देखी।
तब दया विश्रुत अंतर लेखी॥७॥

शबरी के फल प्रेम सुहाये।
निषाद राज मित्र गुण गाये॥
सुग्रीव को मित्र जब कीन्हा।
बालि बध प्रभु सहज बल दीन्हा॥८॥

हनुमत सम प्रिय भक्त पुरानो।
प्रभु ने प्रीति बिधि अति जानो॥
सागर पार गये कपि लंका।
सिया सुधि लाये हरे अशंका॥९॥

सेतु बनाय प्रभु लंक सिधारे।
दसमुख दल बल सकल संहारे॥
रावण मारि कीर्ति बिस्तारी।
लंकापति बिभीषन भयकारी॥१०॥

सीता सुद्ध अगनि परिखीन्हीं।
पुष्पक चढ़ि सब अवध प्रवीन्हीं॥
कोटि महोत्सव प्रभु तब कीन्हा।
राज सिंहासन आपु नवीन्हा॥११॥

लक्ष्मण भरत शत्रुघ्न सुहाये।
निज निज भाग विभूति बनाये॥
धर्म सुराज प्रभु किय सुख भारी।
लोक चतुर्दश भये सुखकारी॥१२॥

प्रभु के भजन भक्त सुख पावैं।
राम नाम जपि भव तरि जावैं॥
जो कोई राम चालीसा गावै।
सो सब सुख वैभव पावै॥१३॥

॥ दोहा ॥
राम नाम जपि ध्यान धरि, मिटहि कलेश विकार।
भगति ज्ञान बैराग्य युत, करहु मोक्ष के द्वार॥

जय जय श्री सीताराम, जय जय श्री हनुमान।
जय जय लक्ष्मण भरत प्रभु, जय जय श्री भगवान॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ram Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Shri Ramachandra Kripalu Kar, Man Nivaas Hamaar.
Aatur Aarat Jan Bhaye, Prabhu Tajo Matibhram Bhaar.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Raam Roop Atulit Baani.
Jai Seetapati Jag Hitkaari.
Jai Sur Muni Jan Sukhdaayak.
Jai Dashrath Putra Prabhu Naayak.||1||

Jai Kausalya Maata Ke Laala.
Jai Dashrath Priya Pratipaala.
Jai Sukh Dhaam Raam Raghuraai.
Jai Lakshman Priya Sakal Sukhdaai.||2||

Naam Raam Raghunaath Kahaavau.
Tribhuvan Mein Prabhutaai Gaavau.
Kausal Desh Mathura Nivaasi.
Raghukul Tilak Dashrath Sut Raasi.||3||

Vishwamitra Makh Raksha Keenhi.
Taadaka Maari Sukeerti Naveenhi.
Gautam Naari Shraap Jab Paayo.
Paadraj Dhari Uddhaar Karaayo.||4||

Janakpur Mein Dhanush Chadhaayo.
Seeta Ji Ka Paani Grihaayo.
Bhaargav Ke Sab Garv Utaare.
Taat Vachan Hit Ban Pagu Dhaare.||5||

Panchvati Mein Dera Keenha.
Shoorpanakha Naasika Biheena.
Khar Dooshan Trishira Sanhaare.
Sab Khal Dal Prabhu Aapu Nivaare.||6||

Maarich Mrig Prapanch Rachaayo.
Seeta Hari Daskaandh Lai Jaayo.
Jataayu Ki Gati Prabhu Jab Dekhi.
Tab Daya Vishrut Antar Lekhi.||7||

Shabari Ke Phal Prem Suhaaye.
Nishaad Raaj Mitra Gun Gaaye.
Sugreev Ko Mitra Jab Keenha.
Baali Badh Prabhu Sahaj Bal Deenha.||8||

Hanumat Sam Priya Bhakt Puraano.
Prabhu Ne Preeti Bidhi Ati Jaano.
Saagar Paar Gaye Kapi Lanka.
Siya Sudhi Laaye Hare Ashanka.||9||

Setu Banaay Prabhu Lank Sidhaare.
Dasmukh Dal Bal Sakal Sanhaare.
Raavan Maari Keerti Bistaari.
Lankapati Bibheeshan Bhaykaari.||10||

Seeta Suddh Agni Parikheenhi.
Pushpak Chadhi Sab Avadh Praveenhi.
Koti Mahotsav Prabhu Tab Keenha.
Raaj Sinhaasan Aapu Naveenha.||11||

Lakshman Bharat Shatrughn Suhaaye.
Nij Nij Bhaag Vibhooti Banaaye.
Dharm Suraaj Prabhu Kiy Sukh Bhaari.
Lok Chaturdash Bhaye Sukhkaari.||12||

Prabhu Ke Bhajan Bhakt Sukh Paavain.
Raam Naam Japi Bhav Tari Jaavain.
Jo Koi Raam Chalisa Gaavai.
So Sab Sukh Vaibhav Paavai.||13||

|| Doha ||
Raam Naam Japi Dhyaan Dhari, Mitahi Kalesh Vikaar.
Bhagati Gyaan Bairaagya Yut, Karahu Moksha Ke Dwaar.

Jai Jai Shri Seetaraam, Jai Jai Shri Hanumaan.
Jai Jai Lakshman Bharat Prabhu, Jai Jai Shri Bhagwaan.
hindiMeaning: श्री राम चालीसा में भगवान श्री राम के सम्पूर्ण जीवन चरित्र का वर्णन चालीसा शैली में किया गया है। उनके जन्म से लेकर अयोध्या में राज्याभिषेक तक - कौशल्या के पुत्र, विश्वामित्र के यज्ञ की रक्षा, अहल्या उद्धार, सीता स्वयंवर, वनवास, पंचवटी निवास, सीताहरण, जटायू का बलिदान, शबरी का प्रेम, सुग्रीव मैत्री, हनुमान की भक्ति, लंका विजय, रावण वध और राम राज्य की स्थापना - सभी प्रमुख प्रसंगों का सुंदर वर्णन है। जो राम चालीसा गाता है वह सब सुख और वैभव प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Invokes the complete blessings of lord rama
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sorrows and mental afflictions
Invokes the blessing of devotion, knowledge, and detachment
Traditionally associated with prosperity and auspiciousness to life
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins through the power of rama's name
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. The Ram Chalisa, known in Sanskrit as श्री राम चालीसा, is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn in praise of Lord Rama. This revered text originates from Traditional. Regular recitation is believed to remove all sorrows and mental afflictions, bring prosperity and auspiciousness to life, and grant the complete blessings of Lord Rama. This prayer is traditionally recited on Sunday (Ravivar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Ram Navami to invoke divine grace. With 13 verses and a reading time of about 12 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Ram Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ram Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Forty verses narrating the complete life of Lord Rama for devotion and peace.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shri Ram Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shri Ram Chalisa is a 40-verse devotional hymn (chaupais) in praise of Lord Rama, the seventh avatar of Vishnu and the central hero of the Ramayana. The word 'chalisa' comes from the Hindi 'chalis,' meaning forty, referring to its forty main chaupai verses framed by opening and closing dohas (couplets). The Chalisa narrates Rama's birth in Ayodhya, his exile, the slaying of Ravana, the rescue of Sita, the coronation as king of Ayodhya, and his identity as the supreme Brahman of Vaishnava theology. (Source: Indif; Bhakti Marg.)

seoFaqs.question: Was the Ram Chalisa composed by Tulsidas?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ram Chalisa belongs to the broader Tulsidas devotional tradition of 16th-17th century Awadhi bhakti, the same tradition that gave us the Ramcharitmanas, the Hanuman Chalisa, the Vinaya Patrika, and the Krishna Gitavali. While the Hanuman Chalisa is firmly attributed to Goswami Tulsidas (1532–1623), the Ram Chalisa as we know it today is part of the same lineage of Awadhi-Hindi Ramabhakti literature, with multiple traditional recensions in circulation. It is recited alongside the Hanuman Chalisa as a paired daily devotion. (Source: Indif; Bhakti Marg.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Ram Chalisa be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional practice recommends daily recitation in the morning after a bath, facing east, before an image of Rama-Sita-Lakshmana-Hanuman. Ram Navami (Chaitra Shukla Navami, the appearance day of Rama), Vivaha Panchami (the marriage anniversary of Rama and Sita), Diwali (commemorating Rama's return to Ayodhya), and Ekadashi days are considered particularly auspicious. Many devotees in North India recite the Ram Chalisa during Ramayana parayana week or as part of an akhanda Ramayana path. (Source: Indif; Bhakti Marg.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Ram Chalisa different from the Ramcharitmanas?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ramcharitmanas is Tulsidas's monumental retelling of the Ramayana in seven kandas (books), spanning thousands of verses and taking days to recite in full. The Ram Chalisa, by contrast, is a compact 40-verse devotional summary that condenses Rama's story and qualities into a hymn that can be sung in five to seven minutes. The Chalisa serves as the daily, manageable devotional practice for householders, while the Ramcharitmanas is the comprehensive scripture for parayana, katha, and theological study. (Source: Bhakti Marg; Indif.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do devotees seek from the Ram Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional sources state that disciplined recitation of the Ram Chalisa removes fear, confers mental peace, dispels obstacles, brings family harmony, and grants the devotee the protection of Rama and Hanuman. The Chalisa names Rama as Patita-pavana (purifier of the fallen), Karuna-nidhana (ocean of compassion), and Ananta-shaktimanta (boundlessly powerful), and devotees turn to these epithets in times of grief, illness, or moral confusion. Recitation alongside the Hanuman Chalisa is considered especially potent. (Source: Indif; Bhakti Marg.)
